reference_population: Top 32 RBs by offensive snaps, min 6 games
output_population: All DET RBs with 1+ offensive snaps

stat_labels.ryoe_per_att_z: Rush yards over expected per attempt

rush_yards_z.how: Sum of PBP yards_gained on run plays where this player was the rusher, z-scored against the reference population (top 32 RBs by snaps).
rush_yards_z.limits: Pure volume. Rewards workhorse usage more than efficiency.

rush_tds_z.how: Count of rushes that resulted in a touchdown.
rush_tds_z.limits: Small-integer stat. A goal-line back can post big numbers without being a strong overall runner.

carries_z.how: Count of rush plays where this player was the rusher.
carries_z.limits: Opportunity stat. Tells you who got the ball, not who did best with it.

receptions_z.what: Total raw receptions (as a pass catcher, not a rusher).
receptions_z.how: Count of complete passes where this RB was the receiver.
receptions_z.limits: Volume — favors dual-threat backs regardless of efficiency.

rec_yards_z.what: Total receiving yards for this RB.
rec_yards_z.how: Sum of receiving_yards on this player's pass targets.
rec_yards_z.limits: Depends on how much the offense uses the RB as a receiver. Reggie Bush-style usage vs. pure grinder.

rec_tds_z.how: Count of TDs on this player's pass targets.
rec_tds_z.limits: Small integer samples are noisy for TDs.

yards_per_carry_z.limits: Doesn't distinguish 4 yards on first down (good) from 4 yards on 3rd-and-8 (bad). Heavily influenced by blocking.

rush_success_rate_z.what: Percentage of carries that were "successful" by EPA standards.
rush_success_rate_z.how: nflverse defines success as meeting an EPA threshold that varies by down/distance; we take the mean across this player's carries.
rush_success_rate_z.limits: The binary threshold smooths over near-misses. A 3-yard gain on 3rd-and-4 is a failure; a 3-yard gain on 3rd-and-2 is a success.

carries_per_game_z.limits: A workhorse metric. Committee backs get penalized; it's not their fault the team uses two backs.

snap_share_z.what: Share of team offensive snaps played.
snap_share_z.limits: Team snap counts vary — 65/game is an estimate. A back on a pass-happy team plays more snaps per carry.

touches_per_game_z.limits: Conflates rushing and receiving workloads as if they're equivalent. A catch and a carry both count as one touch.

targets_per_game_z.limits: Measures how often the offense looks your way as a receiver, not how well you catch.

explosive_run_rate_z.what: Percentage of carries that gained 10+ yards.
explosive_run_rate_z.limits: Requires both the line AND the back. Hard to attribute a breakaway to one or the other.

explosive_15_rate_z.what: Percentage of carries that gained 15+ yards.
explosive_15_rate_z.limits: Higher threshold = noisier for small samples. A back with 80 carries needs 2-3 hits to look "explosive."

rz_carry_share_z.what: Share of this player's carries that came inside the 20.
rz_carry_share_z.limits: Measures trust in scoring position, but volume backs may have lower rates even though they get more red zone work overall.

goal_line_td_rate_z.limits: Tiny samples. Most backs have fewer than 10 goal-line carries in a season.

short_yardage_conv_rate_z.limits: Small samples plus a lot depends on blocking. Short-yardage success is often a line stat as much as a back stat.

rec_yards_per_target_z.what: Average yards per target when targeted.
rec_yards_per_target_z.limits: Drops count as zeros. Screen passes and outlet dumps count the same as downfield targets.

yac_per_reception_z.what: Average yards after catch on receptions.
yac_per_reception_z.limits: Influenced by scheme (screens get big YAC) and blocking, not pure RB skill.

broken_tackles_per_att_z.what: Average broken tackles per rushing attempt, from FTN charting.
broken_tackles_per_att_z.how: FTN charts each play and flags whether a tackle was broken; we take total broken tackles / attempts.
broken_tackles_per_att_z.limits: Counting broken tackles is subjective — different charters draw the line differently. FTN is one of the most reliable sources but it's still human judgment.

yards_before_contact_per_att_z.what: Average yards gained before first defender contact.
yards_before_contact_per_att_z.how: From FTN/PFR charting: yards before contact / attempts.
yards_before_contact_per_att_z.limits: This is mostly an offensive line stat disguised as a runner stat. A great line gives a mediocre back a great YBC average.

yards_after_contact_per_att_z.what: Average yards gained after first defender contact.
yards_after_contact_per_att_z.how: From FTN/PFR charting: yards after contact / attempts.
yards_after_contact_per_att_z.limits: Pure RB skill metric — what happens when someone gets their hands on you. Still dependent on which defenders are making first contact.

epa_per_rush_z.what: Expected Points Added per rushing attempt.
epa_per_rush_z.how: mean of nflverse EPA across this player's carries.
epa_per_rush_z.limits: EPA is modeled from historical down/distance/field-position outcomes. Trusts that model.

rec_epa_per_target_z.what: Expected Points Added per receiving target.
rec_epa_per_target_z.how: mean of nflverse EPA across this player's targets as a receiver.
rec_epa_per_target_z.limits: Same EPA-model trust issue as above.

ryoe_per_att_z.what: Rush Yards Over Expected per attempt.
ryoe_per_att_z.how: NFL Next Gen Stats computes expected yards from tracking data (blocking, box count, space around the runner). This stat is actual_yards - expected_yards, averaged per attempt.
ryoe_per_att_z.limits: NGS model is proprietary — we can't audit it. Small-sample backs can have unstable RYOE. But it's the best available "adjust for the line" metric from free data.
